prerequisites: A Box account with access to the Box Developer Console (https://app.box.com/developers/console), with two-factor authentication enabled, which is required to view the application's client_secret.
A Box Platform application created in the Developer Console using the Client Credentials Grant (CCG) authentication method, providing the client_id and client_secret values.

The Box application authorized for use with your enterprise by an administrator in the Box Admin Console before CCG authentication will work; the app must be reauthorized after any configuration change.

The Box subject the connector authenticates as: set box_subject_type to 'enterprise' (with box_subject_id set to your enterprise ID) or 'user' (with box_subject_id set to the user ID). Authenticating as a user requires 'Generate User Access Tokens' enabled in the Developer Console, and managed users additionally require 'App + Enterprise Access'.
The box_folder_id of the Box folder whose files you want to extract. Set is_recursive to control whether subfolders are traversed.

To use the optional AI fields (ask_ai_prompt, extract_ai_prompt, extract_structured_ai_fields), access to the Box AI API endpoints (/ai/ask, /ai/extract, /ai/extract_structured) for the authenticated account.

authMethods.name: Client Credentials Grant (CCG)
authMethods.summary: Box server authentication using a Platform application's client_id and client_secret (client_secret is the connector's secret field). The connector requests an access token by presenting these credentials, scoped by box_subject_type ('enterprise' or 'user') and box_subject_id. The app must be authorized in the Box Admin Console before tokens can be issued.

setupSteps.title: Create a Box Platform app with Client Credentials Grant
setupSteps.body: In the Box Developer Console (https://app.box.com/developers/console), create a Platform application and select Client Credentials Grant (Server Authentication) as the authentication method. Enable two-factor authentication on your account so you can view credentials.

setupSteps.title: Copy the client_id and client_secret
setupSteps.body: Open the application's Configuration tab and copy the Client ID into client_id and the Client Secret into client_secret. Keep the client_secret confidential; never share it publicly or commit it to source control.

setupSteps.title: Enable the access scope you need
setupSteps.body: To authenticate as the service account, set box_subject_type to 'enterprise'. To authenticate as a user, enable 'Generate User Access Tokens' in the Configuration tab (and 'App + Enterprise Access' for managed users), then set box_subject_type to 'user'.

setupSteps.title: Authorize the app in the Box Admin Console
setupSteps.body: Have a Box administrator authorize the application for the enterprise in the Admin Console (Apps / Custom Apps Manager) using the application's Client ID. CCG token requests fail until this authorization is in place; reauthorize after changing the app's configuration.

setupSteps.title: Set the subject and folder configuration
setupSteps.body: Set box_subject_id to the enterprise ID (for box_subject_type 'enterprise') or the user ID (for box_subject_type 'user'). Set box_folder_id to the folder to extract from, and set is_recursive to indicate whether to traverse subfolders.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional Box AI extraction
setupSteps.body: If you want AI-driven extraction, set ask_ai_prompt to query content via Box AI, extract_ai_prompt for freeform extraction, or extract_structured_ai_fields for structured field extraction. These rely on the Box AI endpoints (/ai/ask, /ai/extract, /ai/extract_structured). Leave them empty to skip AI extraction.

sources.title: Box AI API guide (Ask / Extract / Extract Structured)
